transcript:
[Meeting date: 2026-07-13]
[00:00] You: How did the client presentation go last week?
[00:20] Speaker 1: It went well. We talked about doing a custom dashboard for them — it was on the agenda but we didn't make any decisions.
[00:50] Speaker 2: Right, we walked through the concept. They seemed interested but said they needed to check with their team.
[01:20] Speaker 1: We also talked about a potential integration with their CRM. That was raised as a future idea, not for this contract.
[01:50] You: So nothing committed?
[02:00] Speaker 1: Nothing committed from that meeting. But I'm going to follow up with them tomorrow, July 14th, to see if they want to move forward on anything.
[02:30] Speaker 2: Good idea. I'll put together a scope estimate for the custom dashboard in case they ask. I'll have it ready by July 16th.
[02:55] You: Great.

adversarial_injections.description: Building a custom dashboard for the client was discussed but not committed — the client needed to check with their team first; this is adjacent to Speaker 2's real estimate commitment
CRM integration was raised as a future idea, explicitly not part of current contract scope — past tense mention, not a new commitment

action_items.description: Follow up with client about moving forward on a custom dashboard or integration
action_items.source_quote: I'm going to follow up with them tomorrow, July 14th, to see if they want to move forward on anything.

action_items.description: Prepare scope estimate for custom dashboard in case client asks
action_items.source_quote: I'll put together a scope estimate for the custom dashboard in case they ask. I'll have it ready by July 16th.
